event: from a recent cactus plant injury
admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU)
Liver Function Test (LFT)- Total Bilirubin (Bili) 99 (<17 μmol/L)
Antibiotics: Piperacillin-Tazobactam IV 4.5g stat and then ceased
Source control: Emergency left lower limb fasciotomy
for high suspicion of necrotizing fasciitis
Intraoperatively, he had an extensive left foot
There were large areas of unviable skin
Histopathology was undertaken to confirm diagnosis
and his blood gas showed severe acidosis
requiring left internal jugular vascath insertion
in preparation for continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT)
Initial blood cultures (BCs) isolated Group B Streptococcus pneumoniae (GBSPn)
Intravenous Immunoglobin G (IVIgG) therapy 100g was initiated
He was taken back to theatre to confirm source control
and the viability of the left stump
noting no evidence of necrotic tissue
2	Remained intubated but developed new onset
of Rapid Atrial Fibrillation (RAF) with HR 110bpm
Echocardiography: Moderate segmental left ventricular dysfunction
followed by maintenance dose 900mg over 24 hours
CRRT (Day 2) – AN69 anti-inflammatory heparin laden adsorbing filter
applied (Oxiris Filter) to dampen the inflammatory surge
Bedside surgical debridement was performed by surgical team
Wound culture for microscopy culture and sensitivity (MCS)
Tissue culture (intraoperatively) had medium growth of GBSPn
despite being challenged with a single dose Furosemide IV 250mg
New onset purpura over the right forearm
along with multiple weeping skin tears on right upper thigh
6	Patient was severely deconditioned and developed
He was unarousable and had no response to noxious stimuli
Remained intubated and ventilated on Pressure Support Ventilation
Antibiotic de-escalation: Lincomycin and Vancomycin ceased
despite being off sedation with a RASS -5
New onset lateralizing signs to the left
upon painful stimuli compared to the right side
CT Brain: No acute intracranial pathology
Ultrasound abdomen: Acute Calculous Cholecystitis noted
8	Anuria was established by this stage
Significant deterioration despite ongoing medical therapy
Patient was commenced on palliative care pathway
Family was involved in this decision
and consent for critical care supportive therapy
The patient was not deemed to be a coroner's case
challenged with combination of Furosemide IV 250mg
Antibiotic regimen: Lincomycin IV 600mg TDS – recommenced
